blocks.speaker: Unknown
blocks.content: A-Amy did…?
Shirone blankly recalled her face.
As a targeting specialist, her speed was undoubtedly incredible.
But 987 points was a record that seemed unbreakable.

blocks.speaker: Unknown
blocks.content: Senior Amy exhibits incredible concentration in real combat. Well, that part's similar to you. No wonder she never lost first place until graduation.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Just how many monsters existed in the world? No—this was magic school. It felt like the whole world was filled with talented people.

blocks.speaker: Unknown
blocks.content: No need to aim for the highest record. Before 987, the record was in the 700s. Even the teachers said it wouldn't be broken for another decade.
A significant gap.
Shirone set his first goal at 700 points, refusing to despair.
Three weeks remained until the exam.
He had to find a way to improve his score by then.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I won't lose. Absolutely not!
Shirone clenched his fists tightly.
Shirone devoted every spare moment to the numerical sequence.
Unless he could count faster than Iruki's computations, the outcome was predetermined.
Currently, he could group 20 numbers at once.
But this was no match for the logarithmic compression of units.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Damn it. What do I do?
Shirone trained through the night, casting photon output in the practice hall.
His zero-to-hundred had improved dramatically, but he could only count to 10,000 in a minute.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Iruki hit a million on the first day. I need to count faster.
He needed to understand the exam format too.
If targets spawned infinitely, scoring 1,000 would be easy, but there was a pattern.
The first step spawned 10 targets. Only after eliminating all would the second step spawn 11.
The steps continued rising for a minute, increasing the number of targets.
Thus, failing to clear early steps quickly made high scores impossible, even with late efforts.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It's all about the zero-to-hundred. Whoever clears steps first can gain hundreds of points later.
Practice ended at dawn.
Sleeping like a log for a few hours before returning to the practice hall became his routine.
During exam prep, integrated classes were canceled, and each class took turns, leaving the practice hall quiet.
Students shared tips or practiced numerical sequences while waiting for their turn on the Image Zone.
But Shirone found even waiting in line wasteful, focusing solely on the sequence until his turn came.
Finally, he stepped onto the Image Zone and poured out all his efforts.
His jagged Spirit Zone flashed with photon output as targets were hit.
Adapting to active magic allowed him to cast photon output without magical actions, enabling him to hit targets without looking.
Final score: 567 points.
Higher than Iruki's demonstration, restoring Shirone's confidence.
Meanwhile, his classmates' confidence waned.

blocks.speaker: Unknown
blocks.content: He improved by over 200 points in a week. What's this? Even with talent, this is cheating.

blocks.speaker: Unknown
blocks.content: He's adapted to offensive Spirit Zone. Magical actions can boost power but disrupt flow. Looks like he's casting at least four times per second.
As fewer students used the Image Zone, Iruki finally appeared, having idled in a corner until now.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: My turn now?
Smirking at Shirone, he activated the device.
His evasion-mode Spirit Zone shrank further, now fist-sized.
The dazzling Spirit Zone eliminated targets.
While Shirone's photon output was flashy, Iruki's speed was equally thrilling.
After a minute, Iruki left without checking his score.
Final tally: 568 points.
Exactly one point above Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It could've been coincidence, but Shirone sensed the Servant Syndrome's calculations at work.
The pattern held.
Shirone improved rapidly, but Iruki always outshone him by one point.
Spurred on, Shirone redoubled his efforts.
His modularization surpassed 100 numbers.
The zero-to-hundred became instantaneous, and his Spirit Zone's performance skyrocketed.
He realized faster zero-to-hundred shortened the Zone's duration, but his endurance could sustain it for a minute.
His scores rose daily, surpassing Class Five's average of 500 and reaching the 600s.
But Iruki always won.
The one-point lead was so dramatic the students grew more excited for his demonstrations than Shirone's.
The final training session before the exam arrived.
Since final checks often reflected actual exam performance, the students' focus peaked.
Shirone gritted his teeth and eliminated targets.
His body seemed to glow as flashes shot everywhere.
The light show ended, and the students erupted.
Final score: 700 points.
Nearing Class Five's highest record, second only to Amy's.

blocks.speaker: Unknown
blocks.content: 700 points is really possible. I never thought I'd see it.

blocks.speaker: Unknown
blocks.content: You have to clear the tenth step within 10 seconds to hit 700. How many is he destroying per second?
Shirone clenched his fists upon seeing the score.
He'd improved by 70 points since last class, finally reaching his 700-point goal.
As he descended, Iruki brushed past him.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Congrats. 700 is impressive. Your growth is terrifying.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I don't want to lose to you.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Kukuku, I see. But don't get careless. The exam hasn't even started.
Shirone snorted and stepped off the Image Zone.
When he turned, Iruki had already begun his speed gun demonstration.
The students gasped.

blocks.speaker: Unknown
blocks.content: Whoa! Shrinking the Zone that small is a skill.
Iruki reduced his Spirit Zone to the size of a bead.
Its speed made even afterimages hard to see.
Bangbangbangbangbang!
Targets seemed to explode on their own, detonating around Iruki at the Zone's center.
When it ended, Shirone paled.
Final score: 701 points.
Once again, Iruki had surpassed his best by one point.
The students cheered, forgetting their own exams in the heat of the rivalry.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: 701? One point again?
Shirone grew anxious for the first time.
His palms sweated, mouth dry.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: If Iruki had gone all out, he wouldn't have been so tense.
But another one-point lead meant Iruki could score even higher.
The exam was next week, but Class Five's schedule was first, leaving only three days.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Could he close the gap?
Swallowing his unease, Shirone left the practice hall dejected.
The students' favorite holiday.
Normally, young couples would pair off, but with exams looming, the central park was empty.
Meanwhile, Seriel strolled through the park, humming as if perfect for a walk.
Having submitted her graduation application, advanced class exams were no longer her concern.

blocks.speaker: Unknown
blocks.content: Amy! Over here!
Amy was reading by the fountain.
Still beautiful, but up close, dark circles under her eyes and a thinner frame were noticeable.

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: Wow, you've lost weight. Graduation class must be tough.
Amy smiled faintly and handed over documents.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Welcome to hell. Here's the graduation class data you asked for.
Records of graduates' grades and specialties.
Since all graduation students could access them, it wasn't cheating.

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: Thanks. Let's sit. Want something to drink?
